description_long: This object consists of two distinct frying pans, each with a round, shallow body and a long, straight handle extending from one side. The pans have a dark, weathered appearance, suggesting frequent use and a cast iron or carbon steel construction. The interiors of the pans show signs of discoloration and patina, typical of seasoned cookware. The handles are metallic and appear to be solid, with no visible grip coverings or ergonomic shaping. The rims of the pans are straight and moderately thick, with no visible pouring spouts or reinforced edges. There are no visible brand logos, decorative elements, or hanging holes on the handles.
description: A pair of well-used, dark metal frying pans with long, straight handles and seasoned interiors. The pans have a utilitarian, weathered look and lack decorative features.
description_view_0: Two frying pans are shown side by side, clearly displaying their round, shallow bodies and long, straight handles.
description_view_1: The pans are viewed at an angle, highlighting the depth difference between the two and the attachment points of the handles.
description_view_2: A top-down view reveals the circular openings and the full length of the handles, emphasizing the interior surfaces.
description_view_3: The pans are seen from the rear, showing the backs of the handles and the exterior curvature of the pan bodies.
attribute_handle_type: Single, straight, solid metal handles with a round cross-section and no visible grip or ergonomic features.
attribute_rim_design: Straight, moderately thick rims with no flaring, pouring spouts, or reinforced edges.
attribute_bottom_pattern: The bottom surfaces appear smooth and worn, with no visible patterns, ridges, or brand markings.
